data.title: Stock index futures show little movement, yields largely lower
data.description:
Stock index futures were almost flat on Friday, a day after investors found some comfort from a bigger-than-expected decline in weekly initial jobless claims.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) were unchanged, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1% and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) down 3 basis points to at 3.96%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.05%.

Major U.S. stock indexes also ended higher on Thursday, as worries about the economy sliding into a recession were eased after the initial jobless claims came in less than expected.

"Barely a week since recessionary concerns rocked markets, investors have regained some poise, with share prices largely recouping the steep losses of the last few days," Richard Hunter, Interactive Investor said.

"Markets are closing out the week in a Disney paradise after having starting the week completely frozen! The buoyant market mood over the past 24 hours was given a significant boost by a decent U.S. jobless claims report," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The better outturn pointed to enough strength in the U.S. labor market not to suggest that it is not falling apart. It certainly provided some evidence to the view that part of the weak payrolls may have been weather-related effects, Reid added.

Traders were focused on the initial jobless claims report, as they looked to gauge the labor market after last week's disappointing jobs report.

The economic calendar is light today, with only the Baker-Hughes rig count on tap.

data.title: Fed expected to cut rates by 25 bps at September meeting: economist survey
data.description:
Most economists in a recent survey expect the Federal Reserve's Federal Open Market Committee to cut their benchmark interest rate by just 25 basis points at their next meeting in September, pushing back against calls from some big Wall Street banks for a 50-bp cut or an inter-meeting move.

The economists' view aligns with Fed officials' statements that the central bank will be deliberate and patient in setting policy. The Fed speakers also warn not to place too much importance on any one data point.

Almost four-fifths of the 51 economists surveyed anticipate a quarter-point cut on Sept. 18, Bloomberg reported, according to a survey it conducted on Aug. 6-8. That's after the Labor Department released the softer-than-expected jobs report last Friday, which sparked a dramatic stock selloff on Monday.

The median forecast indicated a 10% probability for the rare occurrence of a rate cut before the FOMC's next meeting.

According to 46% of the economists surveyed, it would take a financial shock, such as liquidity problems or credit market dysfunction, for the Fed to act between its regular policy-setting meetings.

"We think financial markets might force the Fed to cut inter-meeting but otherwise don’t think last week’s data is enough of a reason," Stephanie Roth, chief economist at Wolfe Research, told Bloomberg.

Most of the economists surveyed, 60%, described the labor market as solid, though somewhat softened, while 24% called it significantly weakened but likely to stabilize. Only 16% said more significant job losses are on the horizon, Bloomberg said. Some 69% expect the U.S. economy will have a soft landing with no recession.

Traders, though, are leaning toward a 50-bp cut at the Sept. 17-18 meeting, giving that outcome a 54.5% probability vs. a 45.5% probability for a 25-bp reduction, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

data.title: Soho House reports mixed Q2 results; updates FY24 outlook
data.description:
• Soho House press release (NYSE:SHCO): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.17 misses by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $305.1M (+5.6% Y/Y) beats by $1.26M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $33.3 million, up $1.6 million from second quarter 2023.
  • Total Members in the second quarter 2024 grew to 264,540 from 261,571 in first quarter 2024 and by 6.6% year-over-year.
  • Membership revenues grew to $103.6 million, a 16.1% increase year-over-year, accounting for 33.9% of Total revenues.

  • Updated Fiscal 2024 Guidance

data.title: Petrobras posts Q2 loss after tax settlement but maintains dividend
data.description:
Petrobras (NYSE:PBR) -1.2% pre-market Friday after posting a surprise 2.6B reais net loss for Q2 but approving a 13.6B reais (~$2.45B) payout to shareholders in dividends and interest on equity, equivalent to 1.05 reais/share.

Petrobras (PBR) said the payout will be made using 6.4B reais out of funds from 2023 extraordinary dividends that were withheld by the board in March.

The Q2 net loss, the company's first since Q3 2020, was due to a one-off 11.9B reais tax charge and a weaker exchange rate; a previously announced tax settlement should help the government, the company's biggest shareholder, close a fiscal deficit that has weighed on Brazilian financial markets.

Petrobras (PBR) reported Q2 adjusted earnings before items fell to of 49.74B reais, below analyst consensus of 66.47B reais according by Bloomberg, citing weaker margins on gasoline and diesel.

The company also lowered its estimate for FY 2024 capital spending to $13.5B-$14.5B from a planned $18.5B, but it said the revision still represents an increase of as much as 15% from last year and would not hurt its production growth targets.

data.title: Honest Company rallies after showing turnaround momentum in Q2
data.description:
The Honest Company (NASDAQ:HNST) rallied in early trading on Friday after the retailer posted a beat-and-raise quarter.

The company reported that revenue was up 10% year-over-year to $93 million, driven by strong performance across the baby products and wipes portfolios. Gross margin increased to 38.3% of sales from 27.1% a year ago. The margin improvement was driven by improvements across the entire cost structure, including supply chain and product costs, as well as price increases and efficient trade spend.

Morgan Stanley noted the quarter featured solid progress and said it was encouraged by HNST's turnaround progress. Looking ahead, analyst Dara Mohsenian and his team and see a continued solid long-term growth runway, but kept HNST rated at Equal Weight with risk from macro and competitive pressure seen for the near term.

Telsey Advisory Group analyst Dana Telsey also noted that operating momentum appears to be building. "HNST’s recent top- and bottom-line results are indicative that the Transformation Initiative’s efforts are bearing fruit," she wrote.

Shares of Honest Company (HNST) were up 10.8% in the premarket trading session to $3.24 vs. the 52-week range of $1.06 to $4.89.

The Honest Company, founded in 2012 by Jessica Alba, Christopher Gavigan, Brian Lee, and Sean Kane, emerged from Alba's desire for safer consumer products after her own experiences with harsh chemicals during her first pregnancy. Initially offering baby and household products, the company quickly expanded its range, emphasizing eco-friendly and non-toxic ingredients. By 2015, it was valued at $1.7 billion, but faced challenges including lawsuits over product claims. Despite those hurdles, The Honest Company went public in 2021, raising $412.8 million in its IPO at $16 per share.

data.title: Merck to buy experimental B-cell depletion therapy for $700M in cash upfront
data.description:
Merck (NYSE:MRK) has agreed to acquire an investigational B-cell depletion therapy, CN201, from Shanghai-based biopharma company Curon Biopharmaceutical for an upfront payment of $700M in cash.

CN201 is a clinical-stage bispecific antibody with potential applications in B-cell malignancies and autoimmune diseases. The drug is currently being evaluated in Phase 1 and Phase 1b/2 clinical trials for the treatment of relapsed or refractory non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma and relapsed or refractory B-cell acute lymphocytic leukemia, respectively.

Merck (MRK) will acquire full global rights to CN201 and incur a pre-tax charge of around $750M, reflecting the upfront payment, in the quarter that the transaction closes. The drugmaker plans to evaluate CN201 as a treatment for B-cell malignancies as well as investigate its potential to provide a novel, scalable option for the treatment of autoimmune diseases.

The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter of 2024 and be accounted for as an asset acquisition. Curon is also eligible to receive up to $600M in milestone payments associated with the development and regulatory approval of CN201.

data.title: P10 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 beats by $0.04, revenue of $71.1M beats by $5.22M
data.description:
• P10 press release (NYSE:PX): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $71.1M (+13.8% Y/Y) beats by $5.22M.
  • Fee-Related Revenue: $68.3 million, a 12% increase year over year.
  • Fee-Paying Assets Under Management: $23.8 billion, an 8% increase year over year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $35.4 million, compared to $34.8 million in the prior year.
